changes_made: Changed 'inventory' to 'config' to align with Kubernetes resource naming conventions
review_notes: The control CM-8(6) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves maintaining an inventory of system components with assessed configurations and approved deviations. The original function names used 'inventory' which is not a valid Kubernetes resource name. Changed to 'config' to better reflect configuration management aspects in Kubernetes. The functions now accurately map to configuration management practices in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Use 'config' instead of 'inventory' for resource name to align with Kubernetes terminology
title: System Component Inventory - System component inventory | assessed configurations and approved deviations inc
step1_reasoning: The control CM-8(6) focuses on maintaining an inventory of system components with assessed configurations and approved deviations. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that components (e.g., nodes, pods, services) are tracked, their configurations are assessed for compliance, and any deviations are documented. This involves integrating configuration management tools and processes to ensure compliance and manage deviations effectively.
